title: Outdoor Encounter cards, from Phil Reed (Tools for random encounters) (Solo RPG Friday)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TtCijTAgvPc

Daniel (@dungeondive) celebrates his favourite content-delivery format — decks of random-encounter cards — with a look at Phil Reed's four new Outdoor Encounter decks on DriveThruRPG. They're a rules-agnostic reworking of the old Steve Jackson / Phil Reed Fantasy Trip deck: the system-specific text has been rewritten generic, so the cards drop into any fantasy game. There's some overlap with the original deck if you own it, but each of the four new sets adds 36 cards, and Daniel plans to shuffle the lot into one monster deck of 120-plus encounters.

To show them off, he deals a twenty-card "island" onto the table and improvises a mini-campaign on the fly: washing ashore to meet a wary goblin, a demon offering treasure to steal a town's sword, a wounded dungeon veteran pointing to nearby ruins, skeletal scouts, an orc raiding party, a hand-trap of giant spiders, a star-worshipping shrine and a broken ring of stones — threads that knit themselves into a coherent little adventure. He pairs them with a deck of mini-dungeons and signs off with a request: someone, please, make an *urban* encounter deck in the same vein.

----

What's your favourite deck of encounter or oracle cards — and would you, like Daniel, want an urban set to complete the trio?
